name: Chocolate Toffee (Scotland)
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 Squares (1 oz each) - Unsweetened chocolate, melted
recipeInstructions:
1. NEVER TRY TO MAKE TOFFEE (OR ANY OTHER COOKED CANDY) IN DAMP WEATHER, AS THE CANDY WILL NOT HARDEN PROPERLY.
2. COMBINE the sugar, corn syrup, and half a cup of cream in a large saucepan over moderate heat.
3. STIR the mixture until the sugar dissolves completely.
4. INSERT a candy thermometer, reduce the heat to low, and COOK the syrup, stirring frequently, until the thermometer registers 238°F.
5. BLEND in another half cup of the remaining cream, which will cause the temperature to drop, and CONTINUE to cook and stir until the thermometer reaches 236°F or until a bit of the hot toffee dropped into cold water forms a soft, pliable ball.
6. MIX in the remaining half cup of cream and the melted chocolate. COOK the toffee, stirring constantly to prevent scorching.
7. CONTINUE TO COOK the toffee until the mixture becomes quite thick: A drop should firm up quickly when placed in cold water.
8. QUICKLY MIX in the vanilla and salt, then POUR the toffee into a well-buttered 8-by-8-by-2-inch pan.
9. ALLOW the toffee to COOL completely.
10. CUT the cooled toffee into one-inch squares.
11. WRAP each square in waxed paper or plastic wrap.
Makes 64 candies.
